name: live_100_runtime
description: Live OpenAI-compatible runtime regression prompts. Reports must store only safe metadata, hashes, compact failure excerpts, and no raw rows or payloads.

cases.prompt: List all files in this folder and show the result as a Markdown table with filename, type, and size.
Show the current working directory, then list the files in it, and summarize how many files and folders are present.
Find all CSV files under the current folder, count them, and return a table with path and file size.
Find all Python files in this repository and summarize the top-level modules by folder.
Read README.md if it exists, summarize the project purpose, and list the commands mentioned in it.
Search this repository for references to sql.query and summarize where it is used.
Search this repository for references to fs.write and explain what safety checks appear nearby.
Find files containing the word planner and group them by directory.
Find files containing both validator and shell and summarize the likely shell-safety components.
Search for configuration flags beginning with AOR_ and return a table of flag names and files where they appear.
Show disk usage for the current filesystem and summarize whether the workspace appears to have enough free space.
Show memory usage and CPU information, then summarize whether this machine looks suitable for running a local LLM.
Show the current user, hostname, uptime, and current working directory in one concise system summary.
Show all listening TCP ports and summarize any ports that look related to Python, web servers, or databases.
Find which process is using port 8310 and explain the result.
Show running Python processes sorted by CPU usage and summarize the top 5.
Show running Python processes sorted by memory usage and summarize the top 5.
Show mounted filesystems and identify which mount appears to contain the current workspace.
Show disk usage for the current directory and list the 10 largest immediate child entries.
Show the size of the current repository and summarize the largest top-level folders.
List all tables in the dicom database and group them by schema.
Inspect the dicom database schema and identify tables that look related to patients, studies, series, and instances.
Inspect the dicom database schema and identify columns that could represent MRN, patient ID, study UID, series UID, and modality.
Show the top 10 patients with the most studies in dicom.
Show patient identifiers and study counts for the top 10 patients by number of studies in dicom.
Count studies per patient in dicom and return only the top 10 as a Markdown table.
Show the top 10 studies with the most series in dicom.
Show the top 10 series with the most instances in dicom.
Count the number of patients, studies, series, and instances in dicom and return a summary table.
Show the number of studies grouped by modality in dicom, ordered from highest to lowest.
Show the top 5 modalities by number of series in dicom.
Show the number of instances grouped by modality in dicom.
Show patients over age 45 with the most studies in dicom.
Show the count of patients over age 45 in dicom.
Show the count of patients under age 18 in dicom.
Show the age distribution of patients in dicom grouped into decades.
Show the top 10 patients by number of RTPLAN records in dicom.
Show the top 10 patients by number of RTDOSE records in dicom.
Show the top 10 patients by number of RTSTRUCT records in dicom.
Count RTPLAN, RTDOSE, and RTSTRUCT records in dicom and return a single summary table.
Show studies that contain both RTPLAN and RTDOSE records in dicom.
Show studies that contain RTSTRUCT but no RTDOSE in dicom.
Show patients who have CT and RTSTRUCT records in dicom and return the top 20.
Show the number of studies per year in dicom, ordered by year.
Show the number of studies per month for the most recent year available in dicom.
Show the earliest and latest study dates in dicom.
Show the top 10 study descriptions by frequency in dicom.
Show the top 10 series descriptions by frequency in dicom.
Show all distinct modalities in dicom and the number of series for each.
Show all distinct body parts or anatomical regions if such a column exists in dicom, with counts.
Inspect the dicom schema and generate a concise data dictionary for patient, study, series, and instance tables.
Inspect the dicom schema and infer the join path from patient to study to series to instance.
Inspect the dicom schema and explain which columns should be used to join patient and study tables.
Inspect the dicom schema and explain which columns should be used to join study and series tables.
Inspect the dicom schema and explain which columns should be used to join series and instance tables.
Generate a read-only SQL query to count studies per patient, validate it against the schema, and explain what it would return.
Generate a read-only SQL query to count series per study, validate it against the schema, and explain what it would return.
Generate a read-only SQL query to count instances per series, validate it against the schema, and explain what it would return.
Show the top 10 patients with the highest number of distinct modalities in dicom.
Show the top 10 studies with the highest number of distinct modalities in dicom.
Show duplicate MRNs if any exist in dicom, with counts.
Show patients with missing MRN values in dicom and return a count only.
Show studies with missing study dates in dicom and return a count only.
Show series with missing modality values in dicom and return a count only.
Show the percentage of studies that have at least one CT series in dicom.
Show the percentage of patients that have at least one RTSTRUCT in dicom.
Show the top 10 patients by number of total DICOM instances.
Show the top 10 studies by total number of DICOM instances.
Show the average number of series per study in dicom.
Show the average number of instances per series in dicom.
Show min, max, and average number of studies per patient in dicom.
Show min, max, and average number of series per study in dicom.
Show min, max, and average number of instances per series in dicom.
Show the top 10 largest studies by instance count and include modality counts per study if possible.
Show patients with more than 5 studies in dicom and return the first 20.
Show studies with more than 10 series in dicom and return the first 20.
Show series with more than 100 instances in dicom and return the first 20.
Show a dashboard-style summary of the dicom database with patient count, study count, series count, instance count, modalities, and date range.
Inspect the dicom schema, then answer whether age appears to be stored directly or must be computed from birth date.
Inspect the dicom schema, then answer whether MRN appears to be stored as a direct column or metadata tag.
List all SLURM partitions and summarize their state.
Show current SLURM queue and group jobs by state.
Show current SLURM queue and group jobs by partition.
Show current SLURM queue and summarize how many jobs are running, pending, completed, or failed if available.
Show SLURM nodes and summarize CPU, memory, GPU, and state information if available.
Show SLURM node details and identify which nodes have GPUs.
Show SLURM partitions, nodes, and available resources in one Markdown summary.
Show recent SLURM jobs for the last 24 hours and summarize counts by state.
Show recent SLURM jobs for the last 7 days and summarize average runtime by partition.
Show recent SLURM jobs and identify the top 10 longest-running completed jobs.
Show recent SLURM jobs and summarize GPU partition usage if accounting data is available.
Show SLURM accounting health and explain whether sacct appears available.
Show current SLURM queue and recent job history, then summarize whether the cluster appears busy.
Show current SLURM node states and identify any nodes that are down, drained, or idle.
Read the current folder, identify likely config files, and summarize what each one appears to configure.
Search the repository for AOR_AGENT_DEBUG_TRACE, AOR_LLM_DRIVEN_RUNTIME_ENABLED, and AOR_LOCAL_FORMATTING_ONLY, then summarize where they are defined and used.
Search the repository for shell safety logic, identify the main files, and summarize the read-only command policy.
Search the repository for SQL read-only validation logic, identify the main files, and summarize how SQL mutation is blocked.
Search the repository for LocalFormatter or text.format, identify the implementation, and summarize which output formats are supported.

Run a read-only end-to-end diagnostic: summarize current workspace files, active AOR config flags found in the repo, available SQL-related capabilities, available filesystem capabilities, and available shell inspection capabilities.
